text.paragraph: ae3.xls package provides the API to create XLS (MS Excel) documents.
Obtain the instance of XLS API.

API provides these methods and properties:
createWorkbook
Creates new empty WorkbookChange object to construct the XLS document.
createWorkbookChange
Creates new WorkbookChange object to modify the existing XLS document.
getWorkbook
Opens existing Workbook object from the existing XLS document.
createBinaryFromLayout
Renders any layout tree straight to an .xls binary - the general escape hatch for a caller that already has layout content on hand and doesn't fit the two specific shapes below.
makeDataTableBinary
The spreadsheet-export idea for a listing: give it a set of columns and the rows of a data table, get back a formatted worksheet a user could open in Excel - headers, per-column typing (dates, prices, links...), a title row. The natural "Download as XLS" counterpart to a table/grid view.
makeDataTableMessage
makeDataTableBinary's result, packaged as a flow message rather than handed back as an HTTP reply directly - for composing into a larger script-driven flow.
makeDataTableReply
makeDataTableBinary's result, packaged as a downloadable HTTP reply - what a page's "Download as XLS" link actually resolves to.
makeFormBinary
The spreadsheet-export idea for a single record rather than a listing: given a set of fields and their current values, produce a worksheet laid out as a label column next to a value column - the export counterpart to a detail/form view, the way makeDataTableBinary is the counterpart to a listing view.
makeFormMessage
makeFormBinary's result, packaged as a flow message.
makeFormReply
makeFormBinary's result, packaged as a downloadable HTTP reply.

This is a static SAPI-style module for scripts to call directly - it is not part of the layout-rendering engine (TargetContextAbstract's Skin.getLayoutDefinition(name)-driven walk that html/pdf/text participate in). That separation is deliberate, not a gap: XLS support is optional (depends on JExcelAPI/jxl.jar actually being deployed), so keeping it as an independently-loadable script avoids coupling the shared layout walk to an optional dependency - the same reason ae3.sys.pkg.base ships many similarly-shaped SAPI modules for its own, more universally-available capabilities.

Separately, ae3.sys.pkg.l2.tgt.xls also now provides a real, layout-engine-integrated WebContextXls - reachable directly via ?___output=xls on any page's URL, no script call needed - built on plain string/grid layout primitives (matching PdfLayoutString/PdfLayoutGrid's pattern), independent of this module. See that unit's own CLAUDE.md for the current state of that engine (deliberately minimal: plain text cells only, no per-column formatting yet).

A read-only jxl.Workbook, returned by ae3/xls's getWorkbook(...) overloads (from a File, byte array, TransferBuffer/TransferCopier, or an ae3.base.BaseMessage) - for reading an existing .xls file, not building a new one (see WorkbookChange for that). Per the "Reading data from zipped xls file" example below: call workbook.getSheet(index) to get a sheet, sheet.getRows() for its row count, and sheet.getCell(x, row).contents to read a cell's text. This is JExcelAPI's own jxl.Workbook/jxl.Sheet/jxl.Cell API surface directly, not an AE3 wrapper - see the References article below for the upstream JExcelAPI 2.6.12 documentation for its full method set; not re-documented here.

Returned by ae3/xls's createWorkbook() (a new, empty workbook) or createWorkbookChange(workbook) (a copy-on-write wrapper around an existing Workbook, for modifying it). Wraps JExcelAPI's WritableWorkbook, writing into an in-memory buffer - nothing is actually serialized until buildWorkbook() is called.
Finalizes the workbook (calls JExcelAPI's write()/close() internally) and returns the built .xls binary as a TransferCopier. Must be called exactly once, and only after all sheets/cells have been written - calling it mid-construction, or more than once, is not supported by the underlying WritableWorkbook model.
Creates a blank WorkbookCellFormat with no number/date format applied - typically used for header/label cells with custom font/alignment/border only.
Creates a WorkbookCellFormat for date cells. Takes a date-pattern string (defaults to "yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm" when omitted/null) - see setCellDate on WorkbookSheetChange.
Creates a WorkbookCellFormat using JExcelAPI's NumberFormats.FLOAT - for setCellFloating.
Creates a WorkbookCellFormat using JExcelAPI's NumberFormats.INTEGER - for setCellInteger.
Creates a WorkbookCellFormat using JExcelAPI's NumberFormats.FRACTION_TWO_DIGITS - for setCellPrice.
Creates a WorkbookCellFormat using JExcelAPI's NumberFormats.TEXT - for plain-text label cells (setCellLabel).
Creates a new sheet (appended at the end) and returns its WorkbookSheetChange wrapper.
Returns a WorkbookSheetChange wrapper for an already-existing sheet at the given index.
Moves a sheet from one index to another within the workbook (delegates directly to JExcelAPI's own WritableWorkbook.moveSheet).

One sheet within a WorkbookChange's workbook, returned by its createSheet(title)/createSheetChange(index). Cells are addressed by explicit (x, y) coordinates - unlike some other AE3 render targets (e.g. PDF's table primitive), JExcelAPI has no auto-wrapping/flow layout, every cell position is set directly.
Whether this sheet is currently hidden in the workbook.
Whether this sheet is currently write-protected.
mergeCells(firstCol, firstRow, lastCol, lastRow) - merges a rectangular cell range into one, e.g. for a title row spanning several columns (see the title-row handling in makeDataTableBinary and the Examples article below).
setCellDate(x, y, value, format?, cellFormat) - writes a date cell. value may be a Date, a millisecond timestamp, or (per ae3/xls's own formatterDate) a parseable date string.
setCellFloating(x, y, value, cellFormat) - writes a floating-point numeric cell.
setCellHyperlink(x, y, url, label?, cellFormat) - writes a clickable hyperlink cell. label defaults to the URL itself when omitted (see ae3/xls's own formatterHref for the base-URL/relative-link handling built on top of this).
setCellInteger(x, y, value, cellFormat) - writes a whole-number numeric cell.
setCellLabel(x, y, text, cellFormat) - writes a plain text cell. The most commonly used write method - this is what ae3.sys.pkg.l2.tgt.xls's own layout-engine-integrated XlsTargetContext/XlsLayoutString/XlsLayoutGrid (a separate, newer mechanism from this SAPI module - see the note at the end of the first article above) calls exclusively, since it currently only supports plain-text cells.
setCellPrice(x, y, value, cellFormat) - writes a numeric cell formatted to two decimal places (currency-style).
Hides or unhides this sheet.
Renames this sheet.
Write-protects or unprotects this sheet.

The instance of DocumentBuilder class is created when you building a new PDF document. It provides a set of methods to work on document's content and meta-data. Each instances of DocumentBuilder has following methods and properties:
The 'xls' package is using the jXL (JExcel) library version 2.6.12. It is published under the terms of LGPL2 license.
